channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 25 in Gaza and Huckabee makes first appearance as US ambassador
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes across Gaza killed at least 25 people on Friday including children, hospital workers said, as the new U.S. ambassador to Israel made his first public appearance in Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ten Commandments, ‘In God We Trust’ in classrooms is now Arkansas law
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders signed a bill into law this week making religious displays in classrooms state law.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, KREX 5

item.title: Canadian drummer arrested on child sexual abuse material charges in California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Joseph Seiders, drummer for Canadian indie rock band The New Pornographers, has been arrested and charged with possessing child sex abuse materials, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 67% of CEOs surveyed disapprove of Trump's tariffs
item.description: A survey of more than 300 American business owners and CEOs revealed that 76% of the group believe tariffs will hurt their companies.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) and other conservative allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC).
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deliberations to resume Monday at bribery trial of Nadine Menendez, wife of ex-US Sen. Bob Menendez
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A jury will resume deliberations Monday in the bribery case brought against Nadine Menendez after a prosecutor told a jury that she was a “critical participant” in a five-year fraud with her husband, former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Slain Texas teen Austin Metcalf’s home swatted hours after tense press conference showdown
item.description: A swatting took place at Jeff Metcalf's home just hours after he was criticized by a spokesman for the family of his son's alleged murderer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana takes a first -- and long -- shot at acquiring parts of Illinois
item.description: Indiana is taking a first step toward acquiring parts of Illinois, though a last step appears rather unlikely.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Sen. Cornyn asks Trump for more funds for flood-stricken South Texas
item.description: Republican Sen. John Cornyn, of Texas, sent a letter Friday to President Trump asking for more help for flood-stricken South Texas, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Video shows doctor with measles treating kids. RFK Jr later praised him as an ‘extraordinary’ healer
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — A Texas doctor who has been treating children in a measles outbreak was shown on video with a measles rash on his face in a clinic a week before Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. met him and praised him as an “extraordinary” healer. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nintendo announces new date for Switch 2 pre-orders
item.description: Nintendo announced Friday a new date for Nintendo Switch 2 pre-orders, which were initially supposed to start on April 9 but were pushed back due to President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs. Pre-orders for the new console will officially begin on April 24, and it will launch nationwide on June 5.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Judges warn Congress that more money is needed for security at a time of escalating threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judiciary is warning that Congress is not providing enough money for judges' security, at a time of escalating threats and chilling efforts at intimidation.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google reveals California’s favorite Easter candy
item.description: Everyone has their go-to Easter treat—be it chocolate eggs, jellybeans, or gummies—but a new Google Trends study shows which sweet was the favorite in each state. Peeps were found to be Californians’ top Easter candy choice.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Man missing in Conejos County since Wednesday, car found Thursday
item.description: The Colorado Bureau of Investigation issued a senior alert after a man was supposed to return home on Wednesday, but his car was located without him on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: FDA hiring contractors to replace terminated safety inspection staffers
item.description: When Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced sweeping job cuts at his department last month, he said that safety inspectors who oversee U.S. foods and drugs wouldn't be impacted.
item.dc:creator: By MATTHEW PERRONE AP Health Writer, Fox 5

item.title: 2 food service employees killed in FSU shooting
item.description: A food service worker was identified as one of two people killed when a gunman opened fire on the Florida State University campus Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: NASA's Lucy spacecraft is speeding toward another close encounter with an asteroid
item.description: NASA’s Lucy spacecraft will swoop past a small asteroid this weekend as it makes its way to an even bigger prize: the unexplored swarms of asteroids out near Jupiter.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida State gunman used deputy mom's former service weapon to kill 2 and wound 6, authorities say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The 20-year-old son of a sheriff’s deputy opened fire Thursday at Florida State University with his mother’s former service weapon, killing two men and wounding at least six others, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ohio man arrested after investigators discover IED, videos, photos of bomb-making materials
item.description: An Ohio man was arrested and charged with possessing a destructive device after an investigation into an IED uncovered a trove of video and photo evidence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: 2 people killed and at least 6 wounded in Florida State shooting
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A gunman opened fire Thursday at Florida State University, killing two people and wounding at least six others, police said. The 20-year-old gunman is the son of a sheriff’s deputy whose former service weapon was used in the shooting, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: Republican State Sen. David Argall asked the sixth grader if it was "appropriate to give pornographic magazines to kindergarteners.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KRON4

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: Check your Venmo, email and mailbox: A payment from a years-old class action lawsuit against Facebook may be on its way.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Colorado man convicted of killing his wife after posing as her ex-boyfriend and stalking her
item.description: BROOMFIELD, Colo. (AP) — A Colorado man was convicted Thursday of killing his wife after stalking her while posing as her ex-boyfriend. Jurors reached the verdict against Daniel Krug, 44, a day after beginning deliberations.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: San Francisco's new mayor is starting to unite the fractured city
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Democrats nationally may be in turmoil, but liberals in San Francisco are hopeful the new mayor's collaborative approach will help solve entrenched problems in a city recently known for its bitter infighting and chaotic streets.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wrong man, wrong bed, wrong treatment: Patient's death prompts medical board to discipline California doctor
item.description: A California physician has been placed on five years’ probation by the state's medical board for administering a course of treatment to a patient that was intended for the patient in an adjacent hospital bed.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, Fox 5

item.title: Red Robin launches $20 Bottomless Burger Pass for National Burger Month
item.description: May is National Burger Month and Red Robin is celebrating with the launch of its Bottomless Burger Pass — a limited-time offer that gives fans a month of daily gourmet burgers and bottomless sides for just $20. The pass will be available for purchase beginning April 17.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Two dead and 6 injured in Florida State shooting; gunman is deputy's son, police say
item.description: Authorities identified the suspected shooter as a 20-year-old student who is also the son of a local sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, KTLA 5

item.title: Former MLS player dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: MLB ump leaves game after foul ball hits him in the face
item.description: Veteran umpire Hunter Wendelstedt instantly hit the ground after he took a direct hit from the line smash off the bat of New York Mets center fielder Tyrone Taylor in the seventh inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Family says ICE agents smashed car window in seizing Guatemalan man who's seeking asylum
item.description: NEW BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A Massachusetts family is demanding answers from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, complaining its agents smashed a car window with a hammer and detained a man who they claim had applied for asylum.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Student accused in Dallas school shooting walked toward group in hall and opened fire, injuring 4
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The student accused of injuring four in a shooting at a Dallas high school was let into the building through an unsecured door and then walked down a hallway toward a group of students, opening fire on them and then appearing to take a point-blank shot at one, according to an arrest
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MrBeast fans seek refunds after Las Vegas event
item.description: A crowd of frustrated "MrBeast" fans gathered Tuesday in Las Vegas to demand refunds for a three-day experience.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, WGN 9

item.title: Here's a behind-the-scenes look of the 'Wicked' Broadway tour as it flies into Utah
item.description: As "Wicked" returns to 'defy gravity' for Utah audiences, ABC4 got a peek backstage to see how the show all comes together.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4
